items.title: Stalked, intimidated, but Annie couldn't break her lease without paying

children.content: Annie feared for her safety from a new next-door neighbour in northern Tasmania, but was told she couldn't break her lease without continuing to pay rent. She was then sexually assaulted.

gallery.altText: A woman sits on a bench on the foreshore

thumbnail.altText: A woman sits on a bench on the foreshore

items.title: AI company set to become Tasmania's biggest power user

children.content: Artificial intelligence company Firmus Technologies will become Tasmania's biggest power user if its ambitions to establish three "AI factories" in the state come to fruition.

image.altText: A high tech room.

items.title: 'Absolutely terrified': Scolyer's doctor reveals fears of experimental therapy

children.content: Just 11 weeks before he died, Richard Scolyer cycled 500km in four days. In his last major TV interview, the celebrated pathologist tells Australian Story the secret to a fulfilling life and his doctor reveals the huge risks he took to try new brain cancer treatment.

image.altText: A woman wears a visor hat and blue shirt while looking up at a man with his arm around. He wears cycling gear. They at a marina

items.title: 'No regrets about pulling up stumps': Liberal frontbencher Jonno Duniam to retire

children.content: Senator Duniam says it is time to prioritise his family after 25 years in politics, and he will leave his position in the Senate by the end of this year.

image.altText: Jonno Duniam announcing he's quitting politics 2026-06-14 10:06:00

items.title: Greens join calls for Tasmanian minister Jane Howlett to resign

children.content: The Tasmanian Greens have joined Labor's calls for Racing Minister Jane Howlett to resign over a failure to answer questions about her publicly funded legal proceedings.

image.altText: Female and male politicians sitting in a parliament house.

items.title: A cafe run by students, for students aims to tackle food insecurity

children.content: It might look like a regular cafe, but a new student-run business in Hobart's CBD has a different goal than turning a profit — a mission to feed their struggling peers.

gallery.altText: A woman smiles for a photo in a mini grocery store

thumbnail.altText: A woman smiles for a photo in a mini grocery store

items.title: Satellite images reveal missing Antarctic ice the 'size of France'

children.content: Scientists say a chunk of winter sea ice the size of France off the Antarctic coast has failed to form this year.

image.altText: Antarctic rock face and ice.

items.title: As work modernises in Australia, some rare trades face decline

children.content: A shortage of locksmiths in Australia persists despite government-funded incentives. The nation's changing labour landscape has meant some specialist trades are not being chosen by apprentices.

image.altText: a man in a navy shirt outside a white trade van on a street

images.altText: Livano Comenencia slides on his knees

articles.title: The Socceroos' greatest ever World Cup gamble just paid off

children.content: With his first World Cup team selection, Tony Popovic produced one of the biggest shocks in Socceroos' history. After 90 minutes against Türkiye, his gamble was vindicated.

image.altText: Patrick Beach punches the air.

articles.title: Cowboy country turns into World Cup epicentre in Dallas

children.content: Japan and Netherlands fans are turning out in big numbers in Dallas, Texas, as the city embraces its status as the main World Cup hub.

image.altText: A Japanese fan shouts enthusiastically.

children.content: World Cup debutant Curaçao enjoys a magical moment in the midst of a drubbing, scoring for the first time in a 7-1 defeat to four-time champion Germany. Follow live.

items.title: Curaçao enjoys magic moment before Germany powers ahead

updates.title: FT: Germany hits seven in Curaçao rout

items.title: Dream holidays shattered in $4.3 million travel agency collapse

children.content: Australian travellers who collectively lost millions after a travel agency collapsed are calling for an overhaul of consumer protections in the sector.

image.altText: A man and woman posing fpor a selfie infront of a resort pool

items.title: Multi-billion-dollar economic policy undermined by 'black cladding'

children.content: Almost $15 billion has been spent via the federal government's Indigenous Procurement Policy, but after a decade critics say it's failed to make a significant difference to Indigenous people due to the practice of 'black cladding'.

image.altText: An Aboriginal flag flying outside of parliament house

items.title: Ireland is a data centre 'cautionary tale'. This is the impact on locals

children.content: As Australia and other countries embark on a new wave of AI-driven data centre development, locals in Ireland share how the runaway growth of the facilities can affect everyday life.

gallery.altText: A group of people protest against data centres outside a government building in Dublin.

thumbnail.altText: A group of people protest against data centres outside a government building in Dublin.

items.title: Palestinians fear 'archaeology has become a tool' in Israel's plans for West Bank

children.content: Palestinians believe there is more to Israel's recently announced plans to develop heritage sites along the West Bank, arguing archaeology has "become a tool, a mechanism, to justify settlement and justify the displacement of Palestinians" across the area.

image.altText: A Palestinian flag hangs above Sebastia.

items.title: Dashdot customers urged to proceed days before collapse

children.content: Emails and messages seen by ABC News show Dashdot's staff were still encouraging prospective customers to engage with the buyers agency shortly before it entered voluntary liquidation.

gallery.altText: Man with a serious expressions sits at a table with a laptop in front of him.

thumbnail.altText: Man with a serious expressions sits at a table with a laptop in front of him.

items.title: Japan to roll out nationwide LGBTQIA+ education for the first time

children.content: The country is taking a big step in its efforts to address gay rights but not everyone is convinced it goes far enough.

image.altText: Woman smiling amongst pink flowers.

items.title: Hamilton claims famous Ferrari win, F1 leader suffers late car failure

children.content: Lewis Hamilton has wound back the clock and announced himself as a world championship contender again, claiming his maiden grand prix win as a Ferrari driver.

image.altText: Lewis Hamilton punching the air with delight, holding the winner's trophy

items.title: Rhinos, a river of gold and world domination: This week's Race Around The World

children.content: From dating in Jamaica to world domination in Greenland, catch up on the films from the second week of the new season of Race Around The World.

items.title: UK armed forces intercept Russian 'shadow fleet' tanker

children.content: The British armed forces have intercepted the sanctioned oil tanker in a bid to disrupt the oil revenue ​that helps fund Russia's war in Ukraine.

items.title: Swiss voters set to reject population cap in referendum

children.content: A projection shows voters prioritising economic stability and the country's ties with the European Union over immigration concerns.

image.altText: A billboard sign captured by the side of the road which says: Fed up with traffic jams! No Switzerland with 10 million

items.title: Police officer in custody after Perth girl shot dead overseas

children.content: A nine-year-old Perth girl has been fatally shot by police in Pakistan after officers mistook her family's car for that of suspected criminals.

image.altText: A silhouette of two Pakistani police officers holding rifles at a barrier or gate of some sort.

items.title: Ryan kicks six as Saints get home in GWS thriller at Docklands

children.content: Liam Ryan kicks half a dozen goals as St Kilda earns a thrilling eight-point win over Greater Western Sydney, after Brisbane's victory in Hobart over the Tigers.

items.title: Luai leads Tigers to epic final Leichhardt win

children.content: Jarome Luai has lead the Wests Tigers to a win in their final match at Leichhardt Oval before it's redevelopment.

image.altText: Rugby league players in orange celebrate a try

items.title: Paddleboarder says shark attack victim he helped save was 'very unlucky'

children.content: An off-duty volunteer lifeguard who pulled a woman from the ocean after she was bitten by a shark reveals how he helped save her life while in a state of "panic" himself.

image.altText: Charlie Verco speaks

articles.title: 'Nothing off the table': Coogee drone ban lifted, shark culls not ruled out

children.content: The renewed focus on NSW's shark program comes a day after a woman swimming between the flags, close to shore, was bitten at Coogee Beach.

items.title: Tuckwell honours late sister with stunning second place at Dauphiné

children.content: Luke Tuckwell has announced himself as a genuine threat on cycling's world tour, finishing second overall at the Tour Auvergne-Rhône-Alpes and dedicated his win to his sister, who passed away last year.

image.altText: Luke Tuckwell holds up his hands

items.title: Knicks end 53-year drought to win NBA championship

children.content: New York Knicks come from behind to defeat San Antonio Spurs 94-90 in Game 5 of the NBA Finals.

image.altText: The New York Knicks celebrate with the Larry O'Brien Championship Trophy.

items.title: Fair Work rejects gas giant's claim strikes would harm the economy

children.content: One of Australia's biggest gas producers has lost a bid to stop industrial action at its Darwin facilities after claiming strikes would force shutdowns and cause significant damage to the nation's economy.

image.altText: Evening / dusk shot of Darwin harbour, industrial site, with yellow lights glowing and cranes in view.
